problem_summary: 2024 MCM/ICM Problem F (policy/network/"sustainability" type): design a project to combat the illegal wildlife trade (IWT) — choose a single suitable "client" organization, name a second collaborating client, demonstrate the benefit/win-win, and predict the project's measurable impact. The team frames the whole problem as a client-selection + persuasion problem: which organization (government, enterprise, or NGO) is best positioned to lead, why it benefits financially, who should partner with it, and how much IWT volume the project can cut.
models_used: Client Triangle Profile Model: every candidate scored on a 3-D vector (Power P, Resources R, Interests/Willingness W), 2 indicators per dimension per client type (e.g. Law Enforcement + Policy for govts, Industry Influence + Govt Collaboration for enterprises, Advocacy + Media for NGOs)
Entropy Weight Method (EWM): min-max standardization then information-entropy-based weighting of the indicators within each dimension (objective, data-driven weights)

Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP): judgment matrices + consistency check to produce a cross-dimension eigenvector E=(1.357, 2.289, 0.322); each client type's composite score S = (P,R,W)·E. Applied across 265 countries, 157 enterprises, 30 NGOs -> Enterprises (1.822) > Govts (1.646) > NGOs (0.502); LVMH chosen with total 0.4157

Win-Win / Profit Model via Affine Mapping: cost L = investment + tech expense, benefit M = product-development income, profit T = M − L. Calibrated on a real Apple leather-discontinuation case (L1 from leather revenue share 4.7%, L2 from avg R&D of 3 analog projects), then affine-mapped (proportional-to-total-revenue transform) onto LVMH -> ~$128.06M/yr extra profit

Complement Vector Model + Maximum Matching: complement vector C = S_all·(P,R,W) − client vector identifies the client's gaps; cosine similarity (Algorithm 1, pseudocode given) over all other candidates finds the partner whose profile best fills those gaps -> LVMH complement (0.56,0.06,0.76) matched 99.9% to Seychelles government (0.35,0.03,0.46)

Impact Model: multiple linear regression (OLS, β=(X'X)^{-1}X'Y) of IWT value on 5 trade-category drivers (fur, food, medical, decoration, experimental) using CITES Trade Database 2015-2022; R²=0.979, F=65.784, p=0.015, Durbin-Watson=2.320, residual normality via histogram + P-P plot, VIF reported

Probability-Weighted Assessment Model: PERT/three-point (five-point) estimation e=(m+4r+h)/6, b=[(h−m)/6]², z-score q=(s−e)/b mapped through normal-curve σ bands to per-driver success probabilities, weighted-summed -> 87.2% probability of hitting the 5-year $20B target

Leslie-Gower predator-prey model (continuous ODE + discretized difference-equation form) with a harvesting term E and environmental-protection parameter k1, solved by Runge-Kutta to embed IWT in a broader ecological dynamics frame
key_techniques: EWM (objective) + AHP (subjective/structural) layered together — objective indicator weights inside each dimension, AHP eigenvector across dimensions, so the final score blends data-driven and judgment-driven weighting

Geometric reframing of a qualitative policy question as 3-D vectors so that selection (max score), partnering (cosine similarity), and gap analysis (complement vector) all become simple vector operations
Real-case calibration/analogy: deriving the financial model from Apple's actual leather phase-out and transferring it to LVMH via affine mapping rather than inventing numbers
Full regression diagnostics suite (R², F-test, t/p per coefficient, VIF for multicollinearity, Durbin-Watson, residual histogram, P-P plot) to make the OLS look statistically validated
PERT three-point estimation reused as a probabilistic feasibility/timeline tool — uncommon in MCM and gives a concrete success probability
Sensitivity analysis: ±5% perturbation of each driver (and the dominant medical driver) showing only 0.047% output change -> robustness claim
Counter-intuitive headline result (the winner is an enterprise/LVMH, not a government or NGO) used as a narrative hook
Deliverable framing: closes with a one-page MEMO addressed to LVMH, matching the prompt's policy-letter expectation
why_it_won: ["End-to-end coherence: a single 3-D (Power/Resources/Interests) abstraction threads through all five sub-models, so client selection, win-win profit, partner matching, and impact prediction read as one connected story rather than five disconnected analyses — judges reward this integration", "Genuine originality in framing: turning 'pick an organization and convince it' into vector scoring + cosine-similarity complement matching, plus inventing revenue indicators (penalty revenue, material substitution), is the kind of creative modeling that separates O-papers from M-papers", "Directly answers every sub-task of the prompt and adds value: names a specific lead client (LVMH), a specific partner (Seychelles), a dollar benefit ($128M), a quantified impact (33.81% IWT decrease to $17.54B), and a success probability (87.2%)", "Real, citable data and tools (CITES Trade Database, World Bank, Kaggle; SPSS, Python) plus calibration on a real corporate case (Apple) give the work credibility and feasibility, which matters heavily for a policy/sustainability problem", "Methodological breadth signaling competence: EWM, AHP, OLS with full diagnostics, PERT probability, Leslie-Gower ecology — covers optimization/MCDM, statistics, and dynamical systems", "Strong communication packaging: clear summary sheet, workflow diagram, radar/bar/chord visualizations, notation table, explicit assumptions with justifications, and a client-facing MEMO"]
section_highlights: The Client Triangle Profile Model (Sec 2) is the spine: it defines the P/R/W vector, uses EWM for within-dimension weights and AHP for the cross-dimension eigenvector, and scales to 265+157+30 candidates to land the counter-intuitive LVMH pick. The Complement Vector + Maximum Matching section (Sec 4) is the cleverest piece — it operationalizes "who should partner with the chosen client" as a cosine-similarity search over complement vectors, complete with named pseudocode (Algorithm 1) and a concrete 99.9%-match result (Seychelles). The Impact Model (Sec 5) is the most rigorous: real CITES data, OLS with a full diagnostic battery (R²=0.979, VIF, DW, P-P plot), a PERT-based 87.2% success probability, and a ±5% sensitivity test. The Win-Win affine-mapping section (Sec 3) is the most persuasive, grounding the dollar benefit in Apple's real leather phase-out. The opening summary sheet and the closing LVMH MEMO bookend the paper in the expected policy-deliverable form.
pitfalls_or_limitations: EWM formula is mis-stated/garbled: the normalized frequencies r'_ij and w'_ij are both written as dividing by the sum of P'_ij (a copy-paste error), so the entropy weights are not actually computed as described

AHP eigenvector E=(1.357,2.289,0.322) is presented with judgment matrices and a consistency check mentioned but the matrices themselves are never shown — weights are essentially asserted, and the per-client (P,R,W) inputs to AHP are opaque

Regression is statistically shaky despite the diagnostics theater: only 8 annual data points (2015-2022) for 5 predictors leaves ~2 degrees of freedom; reported VIFs (4.6-14.2) signal serious multicollinearity yet are waved through; the signs are odd (fur and decoration NEGATIVELY related to total IWT), which is never convincingly explained

Numbers are internally inconsistent / sloppy: IWT total quoted as both $26.5B and $25.6B; LVMH 2023 revenue stated as 861.53 BILLION euros (off by ~10x); profit labeled both $128.06 million and $128.06×10^9; the 'win-win' indicators (penalty revenue, joy-driven education) are listed but never modeled
The Leslie-Gower ecological model is essentially decorative — its own result says k1 makes negligible difference, so it adds breadth but no real insight and is poorly connected to the IWT policy core
Affine mapping from Apple to LVMH is hand-wavy ('roughly proportional transformation with their total values') with no stated mapping coefficients
Weaknesses section is thin and generic ('Long Computation Time'), under-acknowledging the real data and modeling limitations
reusable_patterns: The 3-D profile vector + MCDM scoring pattern: reduce heterogeneous candidates to a low-dim vector on interpretable axes, weight intra-axis with EWM (objective) and inter-axis with AHP (structural), score by dot product — directly reusable for any 'select the best agent/site/policy' problem

Complement-vector + cosine-similarity matching for partner/collaborator selection: compute what a chosen entity lacks, then find the entity that best fills the gap — a clean, citable trick for any teaming/portfolio/coverage task

Real-case calibration then transfer via proportional/affine mapping: anchor a financial or behavioral model in a documented real example (Apple) and map it onto the target (LVMH) to defend otherwise-invented numbers
Full regression-diagnostics checklist as a credibility template: always report R², F/p, per-coef t/p, VIF, Durbin-Watson, residual histogram + P-P plot together
PERT three-point (m, r, h) estimation -> normal-curve z-score -> weighted success probability as a reusable feasibility/timeline-risk module
Sensitivity = perturb each input ±5%, report % change in output, claim robustness — a quick, expected MCM closer
Packaging discipline: notation table, explicit assumptions each with a justification, a one-figure workflow diagram up front, and a client-facing memo/letter at the end for policy-type prompts
